title: Dryopteris prismatica
intro: Dryopteris prismatica, also known as the prismatic wood fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the Dryopteridaceae family. The prismatic wood fern has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to reduce stress and alleviate conditions such as arthritis. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, terpenoids, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with prismatic wood fern include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Dryopteris prismatica is classified as a species of fern within the family Dryopteridaceae. This family is a part of the order Polypodiales, which consists of various species of ferns. The order Polypodiales is further classified under the subclass Polypodiidae and the class Polypodiopsida. This classification reflects the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of the Dryopteris prismatica species. As a member of the class Polypodiopsida, Dryopteris prismatica belongs to the division Pteridophyta, which comprises all ferns.
